title: myrsine niauensis
intro: Myrsine niauensis, also known as the Niuhi or Niuhi tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to the Cook Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its rich content of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Myrsine niauensis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Myrsine niauensis is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
